info.title: Snowflake Analytics API
info.description: Open-ended orders/customers analytics backed by a Snowflake semantic view + Cortex Analyst, used as an AgentCore Gateway target. One tool: ask a natural-language question; the service generates governed SQL and runs it as the signed-in user.

servers.description: Lambda Function URL (set by infra/provision Snowflake Lambda)

BearerAuth.description: User (OBO) path — a per-user Entra token the Gateway injects via TOKEN_EXCHANGE; the service forwards it to Cortex Analyst and the SQL API (token-type OAUTH) so Snowflake enforces THAT user's RBAC and row-access policy.

question.description: A natural-language question about orders or customers, e.g. 'total open order value by region for enterprise customers' or 'show order O-1003'.

sql.description: The SQL Cortex Analyst generated and ran, or null if it could not answer.

rows.description: Result rows (each an object of column name to value), scoped to what the signed-in user may see.

explanation.description: Short natural-language explanation of the answer, or guidance if the question could not be answered.

post.summary: Ask a natural-language question about orders and customers
post.description: Use this for ANY question about orders or customers — single lookups ('show order O-1003', 'who is customer C-002'), filters ('open partner orders over 40000'), and aggregations/trends ('total open order value by region for enterprise customers', 'average order value by tier'). Pass the user's question through verbatim in natural language; do NOT construct SQL or pass field names. The service generates governed SQL over the orders/customers semantic view and runs it as the signed-in user: ORDER rows are scoped by row-level security to the regions that user is entitled to see (customer master data is not region-scoped). Returns the generated SQL, the result rows, and an explanation.

200.description: The answer: generated SQL, result rows, and an explanation.

401.description: Missing or invalid user bearer token (OBO required)

502.description: Snowflake REST error (e.g. expired token, denied grant) — detail carries Snowflake's message
